Title: Zaxinone synthase controls arbuscular mycorrhizal colonization level in rice

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase family; zaxinone synthase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves an apo-carotenoid precursor to produce zaxinone; paper also suggests possible formation of an additional unidentified apocarotenoid in planta
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms a signaling apocarotenoid that promotes strigolactone biosynthesis and mycorrhizal colonization
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for early mycorrhization-associated strigolactone increase; not explicitly established as rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in roots upon mycorrhizal colonization at 7 and 35 dpi; mRNA accumulates in arbuscule-containing cells; also expressed in root apexes; induced by phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in mycorrhizal roots, especially arbusculated cells; detectable in root apexes under non-mycorrhizal conditions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Zaxinone promotes root growth; strigolactones promote early arbuscular mycorrhizal establishment including hyphopodium formation and colonization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots; 4DO also detected in root exudates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsZAS promotes zaxinone accumulation, enables early mycorrhiza-induced strigolactone increase, and enhances arbuscular mycorrhizal colonization; loss of OsZAS reduces colonization whereas OsPT11 promoter-driven expression increases colonization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: zas mutant analysis; exogenous zaxinone treatment; GR24 rescue; in situ hybridization; OsPT11prom::OsZAS transgenic overexpression; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling of zaxinone and 4DO
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Arbuscular mycorrhizal inoculation with Funneliformis mosseae; low phosphate conditions; exogenous zaxinone; GR24; chitooligosaccharides; paclobutrazol
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: OsPT11 promoter, arbuscule-cell-active and also active in root apices under Pi limitation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite profiling (zaxinone, 4DO, ABA, GAs); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Exogenous micromolar zaxinone reduces mycorrhization in wild type and zas; discrepancy between endogenous genetic increase and exogenous supply suggests dosage/context dependence
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Localized expression of OsZAS in arbusculated cells/root tips may enhance beneficial mycorrhization without altering arbuscule morphology; engineering should avoid excessive exogenous zaxinone due to suppression of mycorrhization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GR24 rescues mycorrhizal defect but not root growth defect of zas, indicating separable roles of strigolactones and zaxinone. Authors propose OsZAS may generate an additional unidentified apocarotenoid required for optimal mycorrhization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of OsZAS lowers arbuscular mycorrhizal colonization and abolishes the early mycorrhiza-associated increase in 4-deoxyorobanchol. 2. Exogenous GR24 fully rescues the zas mycorrhizal defect, whereas exogenous zaxinone rescues root growth but not colonization and can suppress mycorrhization. 3. OsZAS is expressed in arbuscule-containing cells, and OsPT11prom::OsZAS lines show increased hyphopodia, arbuscules, and overall colonization, supporting a positive role in AM symbiosis through strigolactone-linked regulation.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase family; strigolactone biosynthetic enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a key carotenoid cleavage step in strigolactone biosynthesis leading to SL precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key SL biosynthetic gene; described in paper as a key strigolactone biosynthetic gene
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in wild-type roots upon AM fungal inoculation at early stage; downregulated in zas mutant after CO treatment
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes AM fungal contact, hyphopodium formation, and early colonization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression of OsCCD8 correlates with early SL increase needed for AM colonization; in zas mutant its induction is impaired, supporting reduced SL production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR during AM inoculation and after chitooligosaccharide treatment; 4DO quantification; literature-supported mutant context in discussion
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AM fungal inoculation; short-chain chitooligosaccharide treatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite profiling plus qRT-PCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful SL-pathway engineering target to improve AM establishment, potentially in combination with OsZAS or D14L pathway manipulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as core because the Results section directly measures its regulation as mechanistic support for the OsZAS-metabolite relationship, but it is not the primary experimental focal gene of the paper to the same extent as OsZAS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCCD8 is induced in wild-type roots during early AM interaction in association with increased 4DO. 2. This induction is absent or reduced in zas backgrounds and after CO treatment in zas, linking OsZAS to SL biosynthetic regulation. 3. The data support impaired SL biosynthesis as a cause of the reduced mycorrhization of zas.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative conversion branch toward canonical rice strigolactones
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative conversion steps in rice strigolactone biosynthesis contributing to 4DO formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Canonical strigolactone intermediates including those leading to 4DO
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Downregulated in zas mutant after chitooligosaccharide treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed and responsive during early symbiotic signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression in zas under CO treatment supports defective OsZAS-dependent activation of SL biosynthesis during early AM signaling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Short-chain chitooligosaccharide treatment; AM interaction context
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR and targeted metabolite profiling; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-target for strengthening SL output and AM establishment, but direct engineering value not tested here
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistic support gene measured in Results, secondary to OsZAS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMAX1-1400 expression was monitored as an SL biosynthetic readout during early symbiotic signaling. 2. In zas mutant roots, CO treatment reduced OsMAX1-1400 expression, consistent with impaired SL pathway activation. 3. These data support the conclusion that OsZAS positively influences early SL biosynthesis during AM interaction.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Signaling receptor / alpha-beta hydrolase pathway component
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive signaling control of strigolactone biosynthetic gene expression through D14L pathway
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Strigolactone biosynthesis and establishment of arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsZAS; D10/OsCCD8; D17; SL biosynthetic module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively associated with induction of OsZAS and SL biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Indispensable upstream signaling component for AM symbiosis and positive controller of SL biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Mycorrhizal symbiotic signaling; likely perception of rhizosphere/apocarotenoid-related cues as discussed in cited work
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots; downregulated in zas mutant compared with wild type at 7 dpg
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root expression at early growth stage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Association of reduced OsD14L expression with zas suggests OsZAS supports D14L signaling, which in turn promotes SL biosynthesis and AM colonization
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in wild type versus zas; integration with published D14L pathway knowledge discussed and tested in Results
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Early growth stage; AM symbiosis context
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating D14L pathway together with OsZAS may enhance AM colonization by boosting SL biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Measured as a mechanistic regulatory component; not the principal discovery gene of the paper
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsD14L is a known essential component of AM symbiosis and positive regulator of SL biosynthesis. 2. Its transcript level is reduced in zas roots, linking OsZAS function with D14L signaling status. 3. This supports a model in which OsZAS influences AM colonization partly through the D14L-SMAX1-SL regulatory network.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Negative regulator in D14L pathway; removal relieves repression of symbiosis and SL-biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Strigolactone biosynthesis and AM symbiosis signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SL biosynthetic genes including D10/OsCCD8 and D17; OsZAS as discussed from prior work
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negative regulator whose removal upregulates OsZAS and SL biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key negative regulatory node in D14L pathway affecting SL biosynthesis and AM competence
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots; transcript downregulated in zas mutant compared with wild type at 7 dpg
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root expression at early growth stage
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes AM establishment when biosynthesized appropriately
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Altered OsSMAX1 expression in zas supports perturbation of the D14L-SMAX1 signaling module linked to reduced SL biosynthesis and lower mycorrhization
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in wild type versus zas; mechanistic interpretation based on cited pathway evidence integrated into Results
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Early growth stage; AM symbiosis context
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential signaling target for AM-enhancing engineering, though direct manipulation was not tested here
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper reports transcript decrease in zas, while prior literature cited indicates that SMAX1 removal promotes symbiosis-related genes; interpretation should remain within this pathway context
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSMAX1 is part of the D14L signaling pathway that controls SL biosynthesis and AM symbiosis. 2. OsSMAX1 transcript levels are reduced in zas roots, indicating that OsZAS mutation perturbs this regulatory pathway. 3. Together with OsD14L data, this supports a model connecting OsZAS to D14L-SMAX1-dependent regulation of SL production during AM colonization.
